title: Smoky Vietnamese Pork Bun Thit Nuong
description: A peppery and comforting Vietnamese dish featuring high heat cooking oil and nigiri-shaping, halal-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit
In a large bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and black pepper
Add the sliced pork to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat
Remove the pork from the marinade and cook in the skillet for 3-4 minutes per side
Transfer the pork to a baking sheet and bake in the oven for 10-12 minutes
While the pork is cooking, cook the bun noodles according to the package instructions
To assemble the dish, place a portion of noodles in the bottom of a ramekin, followed by a few slices of the pork, some pickled carrots, and a sprinkle of cilantro
Repeat the layers one more time, ending with a layer of noodles on top
Drizzle the remaining marinade over the top of the noodles and serve immediately
